title: antiphylla squarrosula
intro: Antiphylla squarrosula is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, native to New Zealand. It is known to have medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Antiphylla squarrosula can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although its preparation and use should be conducted under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
